pattern: Treating the number as its decimal string lets every length-k window be parsed back to an integer and tested for divisibility, with zero windows excluded.
hints: Convert the integer to its string form so you can take contiguous slices of length k.
Each slice parses back into a number; the only nuance is that a slice equal to zero can never divide num.
Slide a window of width k across the digit string, count how many non-zero parsed windows divide num exactly.

edge_inputs.description: repeated window and a zero-valued window
leading-zero window parses to small divisor

stress.description: large numbers with small window widths
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    num = rng.randint(1, 1000000000)
    k = rng.randint(1, len(str(num)))
    return [num, k]
stress.note: k is always valid relative to the chosen number's length
